difficulty_definitions.L1: Simple CRUD / single view / static content. <150 LOC target. One wizard turn.
difficulty_definitions.L2: Non-trivial glue: state mgmt, 2+ screens, one external API or framework primitive. 1-2 turns.
difficulty_definitions.L3: Framework-heavy or stateful: shaders, audio graphs, ECS, multi-agent, persistent storage, network sync, sensors. 3-4 turns.

avoid_list.note: These categories already have 3+ installed/shipped versions each. Only include a new entry in these areas if it pushes into genuinely new mechanics.
saturated: habit tracker / weather / BMI / unit converter

entries.goal: macOS menubar clock app. Native SwiftUI, Package.swift project, builds with `swift build`. Shows current time in big digits when popover opens. Click the menubar item to toggle. No preferences window needed.
entries.notes: Requested by macos-qa agent 2026-04-24. Menubar clock is a canonical macOS small-app — exercises NSStatusItem lifecycle + SwiftUI popover hosting.

entries.goal: macOS quick-notes app. Single window, TextEditor fills the window, autosaves to ~/Library/Application Support/QuickNotes/note.txt on every keystroke. Menu item: File → Open Containing Folder. No multiple notes — one canvas.
entries.notes: Requested by macos-qa 2026-04-24. Exercises native file IO + autosave pattern. Deliberately simpler than a full multi-note editor.

entries.goal: macOS color-contrast checker. Two ColorPicker wells (foreground + background), live WCAG contrast ratio displayed, AA/AAA badges for normal and large text. Native SwiftUI, no network.
entries.notes: Requested by macos-qa 2026-04-24. Novel twist on the 'macOS utility' genre — actually uses native ColorPicker (underused framework surface), and the WCAG math is a clean algo test. Avoids the saturated calculator/clock pattern.

entries.goal: macOS ASCII-art banner maker. TextField for input, Picker for style (block / shadow / double), render result in a monospaced Text view. Copy-to-clipboard button uses NSPasteboard.
entries.notes: Requested by macos-qa 2026-04-24. Native-clipboard exercise — NSPasteboard has Mac-specific ergonomics vs. UIKit.

entries.goal: macOS menubar QR generator. Click menubar icon → small popover with a TextField; as user types, render a live QR code below in an Image view. Uses CoreImage's CIFilter.qrCodeGenerator.
entries.notes: Requested by macos-qa 2026-04-24. CoreImage QR generator is a CLEAN API (unlike the miniApp version that needs a JS library — see flaky mini-l1-utility-qr-gen-037). Good test of whether wizard picks Apple-native path when available.

entries.goal: macOS menubar Pomodoro timer. Click the menubar icon → small popover with Start/Pause/Reset. Single 25-min cycle, tomato emoji countdown text in the menubar item itself. NO break cycle, NO settings — just one timer. Native SwiftUI + NSStatusItem.
entries.notes: Avoids the saturated 'pomodoro' miniApp territory by being native + menubar-resident — exercises NSStatusItem button-text updates which menubar-clock-071 didn't (clock just opens a popover; this one mutates the bar item text every second).

entries.goal: macOS one-shot UUID generator. Single 400×200 window. Big 'Generate UUID' button → creates a UUIDv4, displays it in a monospaced text field, copies to NSPasteboard automatically. History list of last 5 UUIDs below. No persistence between launches.
entries.notes: Tests NSPasteboard auto-write (asciiart-074 tests it but on user click; this one fires automatically). Common dev-utility surface.

entries.goal: macOS battery percentage menubar app. Reads system battery level via IOPowerSources / IOPSCopyPowerSourcesInfo, shows percentage in the menubar item (e.g. '87%'). Click → popover with charging status + estimated time remaining. Updates every 60s.
entries.notes: Tests system-info APIs (IOKit) which previous menubar entries don't. Fragile target for the LLM — IOPS C-API bridging is non-trivial.

entries.goal: macOS link-stash app. Single-window list view of saved URLs with title + favicon (auto-fetched). Add via paste-button or paste-detect on launch. Persists to ~/Library/Application Support/LinkStash/links.json. Click row → opens in default browser via NSWorkspace. No tags, no folders, just a chronological stash.
entries.notes: First L2 macOSApp. Exercises persistence + URLSession (favicon fetch) + NSWorkspace.open. No reliance on third-party Swift packages.

entries.goal: macOS color palette builder. Two-pane layout: left = list of saved palettes, right = active palette with up to 8 ColorPicker swatches. Add/remove swatches. Each palette has a name. Persists palettes to JSON in Application Support. Export current palette as a Swift `Color` array snippet to clipboard.
entries.notes: Tests NavigationSplitView (macOS-native two-pane) which color-contrast-073 doesn't. Exercises Codable round-trip on Color (via hex string conversion).

entries.goal: macOS clipboard history menubar app. NSPasteboard polling every 1s; when a new string copy is detected, prepend to a 50-item ring buffer. Menubar popover shows the last 10. Click any row to copy back. Esc dismisses. Persists buffer across launches via UserDefaults JSON.
entries.notes: Common macOS power-user app. Polling NSPasteboard.changeCount is the canonical pattern; tests the LLM knows it instead of busy-reading the string each tick.

entries.goal: macOS tip calculator window app. Bill amount TextField (currency formatter), tip-percent slider (10-30%), party-size stepper. Live-updates show: tip total, per-person, grand total. Save the last-used percent + party size to UserDefaults so they persist across launches. Single 480×320 window.
entries.notes: Common finance tutorial-app subject — exercises currency formatting + multi-input live binding. Saturated as miniApp but novel as native macOS window.

entries.goal: macOS multi-window markdown scratchpad. Each window = one note with a live-rendering preview pane (split view, drag to resize). File menu: New, Open .md, Save / Save As (NSSavePanel), Export as HTML. Notes auto-save to ~/Library/Application Support/Scratchpad/<uuid>.md every 2s. Dock-shown app, recent-files menu populated. Multiple windows simultaneously.
entries.notes: First L3 macOSApp — tests CLI-agentic Meta.W path (planner + CLI generation). Multi-window via WindowGroup/DocumentGroup is the canonical macOS pattern that L1 fast-path can't reach. Phase WX-A's dist/*.app recovery should fire here.

entries.goal: macOS Mahjong tile-match solo game. SwiftUI Canvas-rendered grid of 36 tiles in a 6x6 layout (subset of full mahjong). Click two tiles with matching glyphs (use SF Symbols like sun, moon, leaf, drop, flame, sparkles for the 6 tile types) to remove the pair. Match all 18 pairs to win. Single window 600x600. Cmd+S to save current layout to ~/Library/Application Support/Mahjong/layout.json via NSSavePanel; Cmd+O to load.
entries.notes: First macOSApp game entry. Native-mac angle: actual Save/Open file panels exporting layouts as JSON. Mahjong solitaire is absent from the saturated miniApp list. ~150 LOC target. Bumped L1→L2 on 2026-04-25 after Mahjong L1 canary failed: NSSavePanel sequences need planner stage, which L1 fast-path skips. See gotchas_l1_macos_native_specifics.md.

entries.goal: macOS menubar Desk Pet idle clicker. Lives entirely in NSStatusItem — emoji or SF Symbol shows current pet mood (😀 fed, 😴 hungry, 💤 starving). Click the menubar icon → NSPopover with: pet status, hunger meter (0-100, decreases 1 point/min), big 'Feed' button (resets hunger to 100), 'Stats' line ('Fed N times'). Persists via UserDefaults across launches. No main window — menubar-only.
entries.notes: Idle game that only works as a native menubar app — cannot be a web mini-app. Tests NSStatusItem-only apps (no main window) which previous menubar entries don't. ~180 LOC.

entries.goal: macOS asteroids-style game using SpriteKit + native GameController support. Player ship rotates with arrow keys (or left stick on a connected gamepad), thrust with up arrow / right trigger, fires with space / X button. Asteroids spawn at edges, drift toward center, break into smaller pieces when shot. Score top-left, lives top-right. Use SpriteKit physics (SKPhysicsBody) for collisions. Wrap a SKView in NSViewRepresentable inside a SwiftUI App scene. Auto-detect connected gamepads via GCController.controllers().
entries.notes: First SpriteKit entry. Native-mac angle: GameController gives extended profile + rumble that web games can't reach. Asteroids variants OK per user spec (not on saturated list). ~400 LOC across 3-4 files.

entries.goal: macOS Whack-a-Mole game with native high-score export. SpriteKit scene with a 3x3 grid of holes; moles pop up from random holes for 1-2 seconds. Click a mole to score; miss = -1. 60-second timer per round. After Game Over, an alert with a 'Save High Score' button — opens NSSavePanel to write a high-scores.json file the user can put in iCloud Drive or anywhere. File menu: 'New Game' (Cmd+N), 'Restart' (Cmd+R), 'Show Scores' (Cmd+H). Single 600x600 window.
entries.notes: Native-mac angle: real file-system high score export via NSSavePanel — not a localStorage hack. Tests CommandMenu (macOS native menus) which no other entry covers. ~300 LOC. Confirmed L2 is the right tier — NSSavePanel call sequence needs planner stage.

entries.goal: macOS tower defense game 'Dock Defender'. Path of enemy 'Finder windows' walks across the screen toward an icon at the right edge (your Dock, represented as a SwiftUI shape). Player places towers (3 types: Slow tower, Damage tower, Splash tower) on grid cells along the path. Towers auto-fire at enemies in range. Right-click a placed tower → NSMenu with Upgrade / Sell options. Use GameplayKit's GKGraph for enemy pathfinding through a grid. SpriteKit for animation/rendering. 5 waves, increasing difficulty. Currency earned per kill, spent on towers.
entries.notes: First L3 macOSApp game. Tower defense is the Phase GC reference genre — fits the dynamic-genre pipeline. Native-mac distinction: right-click NSMenu on towers (Mac-native context menu, not a custom popover). Exercises CLI-agentic Meta.W path because L3. ~1100 LOC target.

entries.goal: macOS turn-based roguelike dungeon crawler. SwiftUI Canvas-rendered ASCII grid (8x8 dungeon, generated procedurally with GameplayKit's GKARC4RandomSource for seedable randomness). Player ('@') moves with arrow keys; walls ('#'), enemies ('g' goblin, 'r' rat), items ('!' potion, '$' gold). Step on enemy = melee combat; turn-based. Goal: reach the down-stairs ('>') on each level (3 levels). Cmd+1-5 = quick-save slots, each saving full dungeon state to ~/Library/Application Support/Roguelike/save{N}.json. Menubar shows current dungeon depth. CommandMenu has 'New Game', 'Quit to Menu'.
entries.notes: Second L3 macOSApp game. Native-mac angle: Cmd+1-5 quick-save slots via CommandGroup — Mac-native modifier+number shortcuts mapped to file-system slots. No third-party deps; all GameplayKit + SwiftUI. Bounded scope (8x8, 3 levels) to keep within L3 budget. ~1300 LOC target.

entries.goal: macOS soundboard app. Single 480x360 window with a 3x3 grid of pad buttons. Each button labeled with an emoji + sound name (e.g. 🥁 'Drum', 🔔 'Bell', 👏 'Clap', 🚃 'Train', 🐮 'Cow', 🎷 'Sax', 🗲 'Magic', 🚂 'Whistle', ⚡ 'Zap'). Tapping a pad plays a short tone via AVAudioPlayer reading from sounds bundled in the SwiftPM resources directory (Sources/Soundboard/Resources/*.wav). Use AVAudioSession-free direct AVAudioPlayer init (macOS doesn't need AVAudioSession). 1-9 keyboard shortcuts trigger the matching pad. Visible "Now playing" footer text shows the most-recently-triggered pad name; clears after 1.5s. No mute/volume; just tap-and-play.
entries.notes: First AVFoundation entry in macOSApp catalog. Native-mac surface = Bundle.module SwiftPM resource path (Package.swift `resources: [.copy("Resources")]`) — common-but-trickier than embedding sounds as base64. The LLM is free to either ship 9 actual .wav files via the resources directory OR generate them inline with an AVAudioEngine sample-buffer write at first launch. Either is acceptable; the test is whether it knows AVAudioPlayer needs a file-on-disk URL on macOS, not a bundled stream.

entries.goal: macOS QuickLook thumbnailer. 500x420 window, drag-target area at top showing 'Drop any file here' (NSDraggingDestination). Drop a file (PDF, image, video, doc — anything QuickLook handles) → app calls QLThumbnailGenerator.shared.generateBestRepresentation with size 256x256, scale = NSScreen.main.backingScaleFactor, type = .thumbnail. Result CGImage rendered in the lower half. Below it, a 'Save Thumbnail…' button opens NSSavePanel filtered to .png and writes the rendered thumbnail as PNG via NSBitmapImageRep. Show source filename + 'Thumbnail generated in N ms' caption. Errors (e.g. encrypted file) replace caption with red error text. No sandbox entitlements needed beyond the dropped file's security-scoped URL.
entries.notes: First QuickLookThumbnailing entry. Surfaces drag-IN (NSDraggingDestination via SwiftUI's .onDrop with .fileURL UTType) + a real macOS framework most LLMs know exists but rarely emit correctly. Native-mac angle: backing-scale-factor handling for retina output (LLM forgetting this gives a half-resolution PNG). Bonus mile: NSSavePanel with allowedContentTypes filter (UTType.png) is the modern path; old-school .allowedFileTypes is deprecated.

entries.goal: macOS folder auto-organizer. Single 600x500 window, two-pane layout. Top: 'Watched folder' read-only path field + 'Choose…' button (NSOpenPanel canChooseDirectories=true). Bottom: scrollable activity log. When the user picks a folder: start a DispatchSource.makeFileSystemObjectSource on the folder's open file descriptor, eventMask=[.write] (fires when contents change). On each event, list the folder's contents; for any new file (compared to the previous snapshot), classify by extension into subfolders: Images/ (.png .jpg .jpeg .gif .heic), Documents/ (.pdf .docx .txt .md .rtf), Archives/ (.zip .tar .gz .dmg), Audio/ (.mp3 .wav .m4a .aac), Video/ (.mp4 .mov .m4v), Other/ (anything else). Create the subfolder if missing, move the file via FileManager.moveItem. Log every move: '15:42:03  beach.jpg  →  Images/'. Skip dotfiles and the subfolder names themselves. Stop watching button cleanly cancels the DispatchSource. Persist last-picked folder path in UserDefaults so the next launch auto-resumes.
entries.notes: First folder-watching entry. Native-mac surface = DispatchSource.makeFileSystemObjectSource (low-level kqueue wrapper) — the OS-level pattern for folder change detection without polling. Common LLM trap: forgetting to keep the file descriptor open for the source's lifetime, or forgetting to call .resume() after creating the source (it starts suspended). Also: the security-scoped URL from NSOpenPanel needs startAccessingSecurityScopedResource() if the app is sandboxed and the user-selected folder is outside the container. The goal explicitly says 'open file descriptor' to nudge the LLM toward Darwin.open() rather than a SwiftUI .fileImporter shortcut that doesn't expose the FD.

entries.goal: macOS image-resize droplet. Single 480x420 window. Top half: large drop zone (NSDraggingDestination, dashed border) saying 'Drop images here'. Below: a long-edge size control (Picker: 512 / 1024 / 2048 px) and an output-format Picker (PNG / JPEG q=0.85). Drop one or many image files (PNG, JPEG, HEIC, TIFF, GIF) → each resized to fit within the picked long edge using ImageIO (CGImageSourceCreateWithURL + CGImageDestinationAddImageAndMetadata with the kCGImageDestinationImageMaxPixelSize key for kCGImageDestinationLossyCompressionQuality on JPEG). Resized files written to a per-launch subfolder ~/Downloads/resized-2026-04-25-1620/{originalname}_resized.{png|jpg}. Show a per-file row with thumbnail (using QLThumbnailGenerator at 64px, async), filename, before-size → after-size dimensions, and a green checkmark or red error icon. Total of 'N resized in M ms' footer + 'Reveal in Finder' button (NSWorkspace.shared.activateFileViewerSelecting).
entries.notes: First ImageIO + drag-IN-multifile entry. Native-mac surface: ImageIO is the canonical macOS resize path (better than NSImage + scale since it preserves color profiles + EXIF). Key=value the LLM must get right: kCGImageDestinationImageMaxPixelSize is the proper resize directive at write time — LLMs often emit a wrong NSImage.size assignment that doesn't actually resize the bitmap. JPEG quality via kCGImageDestinationLossyCompressionQuality=0.85. Bonus correctness check: NSWorkspace.activateFileViewerSelecting (singular file URL, not the deprecated selectFile-inFileViewerRootedAtPath which takes a string).

entries.goal: macOS drum pad synth. 540x420 window, 4x2 grid of 8 large pad buttons (Kick, Snare, Hat, Clap, Tom, Crash, Cowbell, Rim). Each pad is bound to a keyboard key Q W E R / A S D F. Tapping a pad (or pressing its key) plays a synthesized drum hit using AVAudioEngine + AVAudioSourceNode: each drum is a short procedural waveform (Kick = 60Hz sine with exponential decay, Snare = white noise + 200Hz sine 50ms decay, Hat = high-pass filtered noise 30ms, Clap = noise burst with 4 pulses, etc). Engine starts on app launch, single output mixer. Use AVAudioFormat sampleRate 48000, 2-channel. Visible 'Last hit: <name> at <BPM-relative ms>' caption. Optional bonus row at the bottom: 8-step sequencer grid (toggle squares per pad). Press Space to start/stop a 120 BPM loop that plays whichever cells are toggled.
entries.notes: First AVAudioEngine *synthesis* entry (the soundboard at 124 just plays files). Native-mac surface = AVAudioSourceNode render block — the LLM must write samples directly into AudioBufferList pointers, not via AVAudioPCMBuffer (which is a CONSUMER-side construct). Common trap: forgetting to call engine.prepare() before engine.start(), or starting the engine before connecting nodes. Sequencer optional because the 8 procedural drum voices alone are L2-shape; bonus pushes it to L2-high. The LLM should pick AVAudioSourceNode over AVAudioPlayerNode — the latter requires pre-baked buffers, defeating the procedural-synth premise.

entries.goal: macOS markdown live-preview editor. Single window, NSSplitView with two panes: left = TextEditor (monospace, plain-text markdown source), right = WKWebView showing rendered HTML preview. Edits in the left pane re-render the right pane on every keystroke (debounced 200ms). Use SwiftMarkdown's built-in AttributedString markdown parsing OR ship a tiny self-contained markdown→HTML transformer if the LLM prefers (regex-based for headings/bold/italic/code/links/lists is acceptable at L2 scope). Wrap rendered HTML in an inline `<style>` block with system-font + dark/light auto via @media(prefers-color-scheme:dark). Cmd+S writes the source to ~/Documents/markdown-{timestamp}.md AND a sibling .html with the rendered output. Cmd+Shift+E exports the rendered HTML to NSSavePanel-chosen path. Window restores with the last-edited file on relaunch (UserDefaults bookmark). Live word + character count in a footer.
entries.notes: First WKWebView + NSSplitView entry. Native-mac surface = NSSplitView with draggable divider (preserved between launches via NSSplitView autosaveName). Modern path: SwiftUI HSplitView wrapping the editor + WKWebView in NSViewRepresentable. The LLM has two acceptable rendering strategies — AttributedString.init(markdown:) (Apple-native, fast, but limited to inline formatting; no code blocks or tables) OR a small regex transformer to HTML (more flexible but more code). Either is L2-OK. Common trap: the WKWebView body color won't auto-track macOS dark mode unless the inline CSS uses @media(prefers-color-scheme:dark) AND the WKWebView's `setValue(false, forKey: "drawsBackground")` is set so the page can be transparent over the SwiftUI background.

entries.goal: macOS Touch ID secret vault. Single 460x440 window. List of saved secrets (label + masked value '••••••••'). Each row has an 'Unlock' button → calls LAContext.evaluatePolicy(.deviceOwnerAuthenticationWithBiometrics) with reason='Reveal secret'; on success, fetch the value from Keychain (kSecClass=kSecClassGenericPassword, kSecAttrService='mchatai.vault', kSecAttrAccount=<label>) and reveal in plaintext for 5 seconds before re-masking. 'Add secret' shows a sheet with label + value text fields, 'Save' writes via SecItemAdd. 'Delete' (with Touch ID confirm) removes via SecItemDelete. Initial secret bootstrap on first launch: pre-populate with one demo entry 'Demo Note: hello-world-2026' so the Unlock UX is visible without first having to add. Maximum 5 secrets to keep UI bounded.
entries.notes: First LocalAuthentication + Keychain entry. Native-mac surface: LAContext biometric policy on Touch-ID Macs (falls back to password on Macs without TID; the LLM must handle both via canEvaluatePolicy check). Keychain trap: kSecAttrService is the namespace, kSecAttrAccount is the per-secret key — common LLM mistake is using kSecAttrLabel which is just a UI hint, not an identity. SecItemCopyMatching needs kSecReturnData=true to actually return bytes; without it you only get attributes. Bootstrap demo secret avoids the empty-state black hole. NOTE: requires `com.apple.security.app-sandbox` + `keychain-access-groups` entitlement — flag in CONTEXT.md if the LLM ships an entitlements file.

entries.goal: macOS text-transform Services menu provider. App registers four Services-menu items via Info.plist NSServices array: 'Uppercase', 'Lowercase', 'Title Case', 'Reverse'. Each Service takes selected text from any app (via NSServicesProvider) and replaces it with the transformed result. Implementation: AppDelegate registers self as NSApp.servicesProvider; methods uppercaseText:userData:error:, lowercaseText:..., titleCaseText:..., reverseText:... pull NSStringPboardType from the pasteboard, transform, write back to .stringType. Main window 480x320 shows: status text 'Services registered — select text in any app and right-click → Services → mChatAI Text Toolkit → …'. A 'Reload Services' button calls NSUpdateDynamicServices() so the user doesn't have to log out/in to see new menu items. Live counter at the bottom: 'Transforms run since launch: N'.
entries.notes: First Services-menu provider entry. Niche but high-leverage native-mac surface — the Services architecture is one of the oldest macOS-native ways to compose tools across apps. LLM traps are dense here: (1) NSServices entries in Info.plist need both `NSMessage` (= the method base name) and `NSPortName` (= app's bundle name), and the method signature MUST be exactly `func name(_ pboard: NSPasteboard, userData: String, error: AutoreleasingUnsafeMutablePointer<NSString>)` — any deviation and the Services subsystem silently ignores the registration. (2) NSApp.servicesProvider must be set in applicationDidFinishLaunching, not init. (3) NSUpdateDynamicServices() is the modern call (forces pbs to re-scan) — most LLMs reach for the deprecated `pbs -flush`. The 'select text in any app' path means the provider HAS to round-trip through the pasteboard with NSStringPboardType (Apple's compat name; UTType.plainText ‘public.utf8-plain-text’ also works and is what to ship).

entries.goal: macOS Frogger pond. Single 480x480 SwiftUI window hosting a SpriteKit SKScene via SKView wrapped in NSViewRepresentable. Frog SK_SpriteNode at bottom-center; arrow keys (NSEvent.keyDown) move it ONE grid-step at a time (40px increments). Bottom half: 3 lanes of cars (red SKShapeNode rectangles) moving left or right at varying speeds via SKAction.repeatForever(SKAction.moveBy(x:duration:)). Middle: a 'water' band where the frog DROWNS unless on a log (3 lanes of brown SKShapeNode rectangles drifting). Top: 5 home pads (green slots). Reach a home pad = +100 score (frog respawns at bottom). Hit a car or fall in water (between logs) = lose 1 life (3 lives). SKPhysicsBody on every node with categoryBitMask + contactTestBitMask for SKPhysicsContactDelegate detection. Score + lives HUD as SKLabelNode. CommandMenu adds 'Game' menu with 'New Game' (Cmd+N).
entries.notes: First L1 SpriteKit entry. Distinct from existing Comet-Sweeper-120 (asteroids) — Frogger needs lane-based grid movement + categorical contact testing across 3 distinct categories (frog vs water vs car vs log). NSEvent.keyDown wiring inside an NSViewRepresentable-wrapped SKView is the planner challenge: needs first-responder chain forwarded from the host SwiftUI view.

entries.goal: macOS Atari-2600-style overhead boxing. 600x500 SwiftUI window hosting a SpriteKit scene. Two stick-figure boxers — player (left, blue, SKShapeNode body) vs CPU (right, red). Player INPUT: WASD = pan boxer in 8 directions (NSEvent.keyDown via .onKeyPress on macOS 14+ OR an NSResponder bridge); J = jab (quick punch, instant), K = power punch (HOLD K = wind-up animation 600ms, RELEASE K = throw — if released within window, +3 hits; otherwise auto-release at 1200ms = released-too-late penalty). OPTIONAL gamepad: GCController.controllers() auto-detects extended profile; left-stick = move, A button = jab, B button = power punch. CPU AI mirrors the mobile-140 state machine (approach if range > 80, jab if range 40-80, dodge if player charging > 300ms) but with tighter timing windows. Hit detection via SKPhysicsBody contactTestBitMask between glove SKNodes and body SKNode. 16-hit KO. Score boxes top corners as SKLabelNode. CommandMenu File > 'New Round' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Native angle: GCController extended profile gives rumble + analog inputs that web can't reach. Tests dual-input (key + gamepad) reactive plumbing — common LLM trap is wiring only one input source. Pairs with mobile-140 — same AI spec, different platform skin. User-spec'd Atari Boxing reference.

entries.goal: macOS touch-football play-designer. 700x500 SwiftUI window. TOP HALF: 60-yard field rendered on SwiftUI Canvas (yard lines drawn with Path strokes). 5 receiver-position dots draggable along the line of scrimmage. ROUTE EDIT MODE: click+drag from any receiver dot to draw an SVG-style polyline (Path.move + addLine sequence stored as [(x,y)] coords); release to commit the route for that receiver. BOTTOM HALF: play library (NSList of saved plays loaded from ~/Library/Application Support/PlayDesigner/playbook.json). 'Save Playbook' button → NSSavePanel → writes JSON via JSONEncoder. 'Run Play' button → simulation: each receiver follows their polyline at 5pt/frame; CPU defense = 5 defender dots that pursue receivers using nearest-route-overlap heuristic; if defender intercepts the ball-carrier (ball is at the route-leader's current position), play dead. CommandMenu File > 'Save Playbook' (Cmd+S), Edit > 'Clear Routes' (Cmd+\). Native angle: SwiftUI Canvas drag-to-draw with @GestureState across drag (state survives onChanged → onEnded).
entries.notes: First sports-tactics + Canvas drag-to-draw entry. SwiftUI Canvas + DragGesture gesture-state coordination is a real planner challenge — LLMs often lose state across .onChanged → .onEnded boundary.

entries.goal: macOS lane bowling with 3D physics. 540x680 SwiftUI window hosting a SceneKit SCNView via NSViewRepresentable. SCNScene: bowling lane SCNFloor (with wood texture material), 10-pin SCNNode tower at the far end (each pin = SCNCylinder with SCNPhysicsBody dynamic + categoryBitMask 'pin'), bowling ball SCNSphere at the lane-start (SCNPhysicsBody dynamic + categoryBitMask 'ball'). Camera positioned behind the ball looking down-lane. INPUT: mouse-drag across the SCNView (NSEvent mouseDragged) — horizontal drag-X = initial ball X position on the lane, vertical drag-Y release distance = power (longer drag = harder throw). On mouseUp, apply physicsBody.applyForce in the +Z direction proportional to power. SCNPhysicsContactDelegate counts pins-knocked-down (pin Y < 0.1m = fallen). 10 frames standard bowling scoring including spare (10 pins in 2 throws = next throw doubles) and strike (10 in 1 = next 2 throws double) carry-over. Frame-by-frame score table on the right side via SwiftUI grid. CommandMenu File > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: First SceneKit entry in catalog. Native angle: 3D physics that web can't credibly do at this fidelity. Common LLM trap: forgetting to set isDynamic=true on the ball OR forgetting friction/restitution on the lane SCNFloor (defaults are too low and ball clack-clack-bounces). Also: SCNPhysicsBody.allowsResting = false on pins or they freeze before falling fully.

entries.goal: macOS Micro-City builder. 800x680 SwiftUI window. 30x20 grid drawn on SwiftUI Canvas representing a city. RIGHT-PANE building palette: Residential (R, 50¤), Commercial (C, 80¤), Industrial (I, 100¤), Road (R, 5¤), Power-plant (P, 200¤). Click-and-drag on the grid to PAINT buildings (each tile costs from a coin balance shown top-left, starts at 1000¤). PATH-CONNECTIVITY: use GameplayKit's GKGridGraph — add nodes for every Road tile, addConnections(to:bidirectional:true) at placement-time. Every R/C/I tile must be reachable from a Power-plant tile via Road tiles (GKGridGraph.findPath(from:to:) returns non-empty), otherwise it darkens (tile.isPowered = false, no production). MONTH TICK: driven by Combine's Timer.publish(every: 4.0, on: .main, in: .common).autoconnect(); each tick advances simulation. Active-C tiles need adjacent-or-road-connected R for shoppers (income +5¤ each); Active-I tiles need road-connected R for workers (+8¤ each); Active-R tiles pay rent (+2¤ each). Tax revenue accrues to balance. Cmd+S → NSSavePanel writes city snapshot (grid array + balance + month) to ~/Library/Application Support/MicroCity/save.json via JSONEncoder. Cmd+O loads a saved file. Window-bottom HUD: month + balance + active-percent + GKGridGraph node-count. CommandMenu has File menu (Save/Load) and Game menu w/ 'Bulldoze mode' toggle (Cmd+B; in Bulldoze mode, drag erases tiles + refunds 50% cost).
entries.notes: First GKGridGraph entry + first Combine TimerPublisher entry. SimCity-flavored (per user spec) but bounded to 30x20 / 5 building types so it's L3 not L4. CRITICAL LLM trap: GKGridGraph nodes need explicit `addConnections(to: neighbors, bidirectional: true)` AFTER addNodes — without that step, pathfinding silently returns empty paths and nothing is ever 'powered'. Second trap: Timer.publish needs .autoconnect() OR a manual .connect() on the cancellable, otherwise the publisher never fires.

entries.goal: macOS EverQuill — EverQuest-style 1st-person grid crawler. 800x600 SwiftUI window hosting a SpriteKit scene with SKTileMapNode for isometric world rendering (32x16 pixel tile size, 25x15 visible tiles). Single character WASD-moves grid-by-grid (NSEvent.keyDown). Turn-based combat triggers on collision with monster sprite: combat panel overlays with ATK / DEF / FLEE buttons. Stats: Str / Dex / Int / Sta / Cha (rolled at character creation). 5 zones: forest (green tileset), swamp (purple), ruin (gray), dungeon (red), lair (black) — each zone uses a different SKTileSet color palette. SAVE: Cmd+S → NSSavePanel writes full state JSON (level + tile position + stats + inventory array + visited zones) via JSONEncoder. LOAD: Cmd+O → NSOpenPanel reads. CommandMenu adds: Game > 'New Adventurer' (Cmd+N), Game > 'Quests' submenu showing active quest log entries as NSAttributedString (status-color-coded: in-progress=amber, complete=green, failed=red). XP system: kill monster = +10..+50 XP based on zone difficulty; level-up at 100/200/400/800 XP grants +HP and stat boost.
entries.notes: EverQuest reference (user spec). First SKTileMapNode entry. NSAttributedString in CommandMenu (used for color-coded quest log) is a rare native-mac surface most LLMs miss — they default to plain Text. Bounded to 5 zones / single character / no party so scope stays in L3.

entries.goal: macOS Typing-of-the-Dead-lite. 600x420 SwiftUI window hosting a SpriteKit scene. Zombie SK_SpriteNodes spawn at the right edge and walk left toward the player edge (left), each at 30pt/sec. Each zombie has a SKLabelNode WORD floating above it (3-letter words for round 1, scaling to 8-letter words by round 5; words drawn from a 500-word embedded array). Player types the targeted zombie's word using NSEvent.keyDown — typing the FIRST letter selects that zombie (highlight in red); each subsequent CORRECT letter highlights green and removes that letter from the word; complete word → zombie EXPLODES (SKEmitterNode burst), +10 score. Wrong letter = 100ms freeze + miss-sound. Zombie reaches left edge — lose 1 life (3 lives). 60-second wave per round. Top-5 high scores persisted via UserDefaults (Codable wrapper). HUD: score + lives + current-target word at top-center (SKLabelNode). CommandMenu Game > 'New Wave' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Typing-game entry. NSEvent keyDown trapping when SpriteKit view is foreground requires .onKeyPress (macOS 14+) on the host SwiftUI view OR an NSResponder bridge — common LLM trap is using a TextField onChange, which loses focus when the SKView is foreground. SKEmitterNode for zombie-explode juice.

entries.goal: macOS Spelling Bee (NYT-style hex pangram). 540x420 SwiftUI window. CENTER: hexagon arrangement of 7 letters — 6 outer letters in a ring + 1 center letter (drawn as a SwiftUI Path with hexagonal symmetry, scaled by min(width,height) so resize works). Center letter is amber-highlighted. BOTTOM: input bar with Backspace + Submit + Shuffle buttons. User TYPES (or CLICKS letters) to assemble a candidate word ≥ 4 letters using ONLY the given 7 letters (with repeats allowed) and MUST include the center letter. Submit → check against a 5000-word common-English dictionary bundled as a .txt resource (Bundle.module.url(forResource:withExtension:)) — valid words score by length (4=1pt, 5=2pt, 6=3pt, 7+=length pts), bonus +7 for pangrams (uses all 7 letters). Score + word-list shown in right side panel (already-found words greyed out). Shuffle button rotates the outer 6 letters. Persist today's-best-score + words-found list to UserDefaults keyed by date (YYYY-MM-DD).
entries.notes: NYT Spelling Bee inspiration. SwiftUI Path for the hexagon is the geometry challenge; trap is forgetting to scale the hex points by min(width,height) so resize breaks layout. Bundle.module SwiftPM resource path is the bundled-dictionary surface — same pattern as soundboard-124.

entries.goal: macOS Pinochle (4-player partner trick game). 600x500 SwiftUI window. 4 players: you (bottom) + 3 AI bots (your partner across, 2 opponents on sides). Double-deck = 48 cards (9-A, two of each suit). PHASE 1 — BIDDING: auction starts at 250 points, +10 increments; each player passes or bids higher; high bidder picks trump suit. AI bidder uses meld-count heuristic: counts potential melds in hand and bids accordingly. PHASE 2 — MELD: each player shows melds before tricks: Pinochle (Q♠ + J♦ = 40), Royal Marriage in trump (40), Run in trump (150), Aces-around (100), Kings-around (80), Queens-around (60), Jacks-around (40). Score added to running total. PHASE 3 — TRICKS (12 tricks): each player plays 1 card per trick; must FOLLOW SUIT then TRUMP; high card or high trump wins trick (+1pt per ace, +1 per ten, +1 per king + last trick 1pt). Bidding team must MAKE their bid (meld + tricks ≥ bid) or go SET (-bid). First team to 1500 wins. CommandMenu File > 'New Match' (Cmd+N). UI: hand fanned at the bottom (SwiftUI HStack with rotation per card), trick area centered, score panel right.
entries.notes: First Pinochle entry. Multi-phase trick game (bid → meld → play) is the L2 challenge — LLMs often collapse phases or skip meld scoring. Saturated-list-clean replacement for Cribbage.

entries.goal: macOS Euchre (4-player partner trick game). 600x500 SwiftUI window. You + 3 AI bots (1 partner across, 2 opponents). 24-card deck (9, 10, J, Q, K, A in each of 4 suits). Deal 5 cards each + 1 face-up turn-up card. PHASE 1 — TRUMP CALL: starting left of dealer, each player either ORDERS UP (accepts the turn-up suit as trump; dealer picks it up + discards 1) or PASSES. If all 4 pass, second round = each player NAMES a different suit as trump or passes (dealer is 'stuck' — must call). Loner option: caller plays alone (partner sits out) for bonus points. PHASE 2 — 5 TRICKS: standard follow-suit trick play; trump suit beats all others; jacks-of-trump-color (RIGHT BOWER = trump jack, LEFT BOWER = same-color other-suit jack — considered trump). Bot AI: simple 'follow suit, trump if can't, lead high if no trump'. SCORING: making team scores 1pt (3-4 tricks), 2pts (5 tricks = 'march'), 4pts (loner with march). Euchred (caller's team takes < 3 tricks) = opposing team scores 2pts. First team to 10 wins.
entries.notes: Euchre absent from catalog and not on saturated list. Right-bower / left-bower trump rules are the L2 spec (LLMs often forget left-bower is the OTHER-suit-same-color jack).

entries.goal: macOS Othello (Reversi) with GameplayKit AI. 540x600 SwiftUI window. 8x8 Othello board drawn with SwiftUI ZStack of LazyVGrid cells. Click any LEGAL cell (highlighted with light-gray dots BEFORE click) to place a black disc; valid placement flips opposing white discs in straight lines. AI opponent driven by GKMinmaxStrategist with maxLookAheadDepth = 4. Implement OthelloBoard as a class conforming to GKGameModel: unscoredOutcome, currentPlayer, players, gameModelUpdates(for:), apply(_:), copy(with:), setGameModel(_:); and OthelloMove conforming to GKGameModelUpdate (.value tracks score after this move). GKMinmaxStrategist.bestMove(for: aiPlayer) returns the AI's choice; apply it and re-render. Score readout (B:N W:N) updates after each move. Game ends when neither player has a legal move — highest count wins. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' (Cmd+N).
entries.notes: First GKMinmaxStrategist entry — distinctly mac-native AI primitive. LLMs often try to roll their own minimax; the test is whether they reach for GKGameModel correctly (which has 7 required methods that ALL need correct implementation or strategist returns nil moves silently).

entries.goal: macOS Connect Four with GameplayKit AI. 480x540 SwiftUI window. 7-column x 6-row Connect Four board drawn on SwiftUI Canvas (column hover-highlight + falling-disc animation via withAnimation(.spring())). Click a column → disc drops to lowest empty row. AI opponent: GKMinmaxStrategist with maxLookAheadDepth = 6 (Connect-Four is solved at depth-6 plays strong but not perfect; depth-8 would be unbeatable but slow). Implement ConnectFourBoard conforming to GKGameModel; alpha-beta pruning is GKMinmaxStrategist's responsibility internally, but the GAME-MODEL MUST return well-bounded gameModelUpdates(for:) (only 7 column-moves) and unscoredOutcome must distinguish win/loss/draw correctly. Score evaluation in GKGameModel.score(for:): +infinity if 4-in-a-row achieved, +N × (count of player's open 3-in-a-row + 2× count of player's 2-with-open-extensions). 2-player local + AI mode toggle. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' (Cmd+N), Game > 'AI Strength' submenu (depth 2/4/6).
entries.notes: Pairs with #142 — both exercise GameplayKit AI but Connect-Four needs a proper score(for:) heuristic that LLMs often write naively (just win/loss leaves the AI flailing in midgame). The score(for:)-must-be-bounded-but-meaningful is the planner trap.

entries.goal: macOS CivMini — 4X turn-based strategy. 800x680 SwiftUI window. 16x12 hex map (offset coordinates) drawn on SwiftUI Canvas with Path strokes per hex; each hex has terrain (grassland, plains, hills, forest, water) randomly seeded at game start. 2 civilizations: you (blue) + AI (red). UNITS: Settler (founds cities), Warrior (combat 2/2), Archer (combat 3/1, range 2). Each turn: move units (Settler 1 hex, Warrior 1 hex, Archer 1 hex), attack adjacent enemies, found cities (Settler consumes itself + creates city tile). CITIES: produce 1 unit per N turns; each city contributes to TECH POINTS (1/turn). TECH TREE (5 techs): Bronze (unlocks +Warrior), Archery (unlocks Archer), Currency (cities +50% production), Mathematics (units +1 move), Iron Working (unlocks +Strength Warrior). RESEARCH: 50 / 100 / 200 / 400 / 800 tech points respectively. WIN: capture both AI cities OR research final tech. SAVE: Cmd+S → NSSavePanel writes full game state JSON (map + units + cities + tech progress) via JSONEncoder. AI uses simple movement-toward-nearest-enemy + 'build settler if cities < 3, else build warrior' heuristic.
entries.notes: 4X-lite. Hex math (offset → cube coords for distance calcs) + tech-tree state + AI move scoring at L3 budget. Bounded to 16x12 / 2 civs / 5 techs prevents scope explosion. Hex-distance trap: Manhattan distance fails on hex; need cube-coord conversion.

entries.goal: macOS Dungeon-of-Windows — each room is a separate NSWindow. The dungeon IS the desktop. 8 rooms total laid out on a 4x4 imaginary grid — each room = a real top-level NSWindow at a specific NSScreen position. Player character lives in WHICHEVER window is currently active. Each room window is 400x300, content = SwiftUI scene with the player sprite + walls + maybe an enemy + exit-doors (N/S/E/W per room). When player moves through a door (arrow keys, NSEvent.keyDown): current window slide-animates closed (NSWindow.animator().alphaValue = 0 over 0.3s, then close()), and the adjacent room's window slide-animates open at the corresponding screen position with NSWindow.makeKeyAndOrderFront(_:). Use GKGridGraph to lay out the 4x4 dungeon (some grid cells are 'no room'; doors only connect existing-rooms-with-shared-edge). COMBAT: when entering a room with an enemy, sprite-vs-sprite battle happens within that window (turn-based: arrow-keys to attack/dodge). Inventory persists across windows via a single ObservableObject AppState shared via .environmentObject across all WindowGroup scenes. CommandMenu Game > 'New Dungeon' (Cmd+N) opens the entry-room window.
entries.notes: TRULY mac-native crawler — impossible on web. Each room = real OS-level NSWindow. LLMs will be tempted to fake it with NSPanels inside one parent window OR with multiple SwiftUI Windows in a single WindowGroup — the test is whether they orchestrate multiple top-level NSWindows via NSWindowController + makeKeyAndOrderFront on the main thread. Common trap: NSWindow.delegate not assigned → windowWillClose never fires → dungeon state desyncs from window state.

entries.goal: macOS Pattern Master — Simon-meets-rhythm. 500x400 SwiftUI window. 4 colored pads (Red TL, Green TR, Blue BL, Yellow BR) drawn as RoundedRectangles in a 2x2 LazyVGrid. AUDIO: AVAudioEngine with 4 AVAudioSourceNode instances, each rendering a different sine-tone (Red=261Hz/C4, Green=329Hz/E4, Blue=392Hz/G4, Yellow=523Hz/C5) shaped by an exponential envelope (attack 5ms, decay 200ms). PATTERN PHASE: app plays a sequence (round 1 = 1 step, round 2 = 2 steps, ...) at 120 BPM = 500ms intervals using DispatchSourceTimer. PLAYBACK PHASE: player must repeat the sequence by clicking pads in time. SCORING (different from pure-Simon): each tap timed against the expected beat — Perfect = ±50ms (+100pts), Good = ±150ms (+50pts), Miss = >±150ms or wrong pad (game over). Combo counter, score top-right. Round complete → +1 step. Persist top-3 scores to UserDefaults. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' (Cmd+N).
entries.notes: Distinct from saturated-list Simon: Pattern-Master's TIMING-graded scoring is the new mechanic; pure-Simon is on the saturated list. Pairs with drum-pad-128 to extend AVAudioSourceNode coverage. Trap: AVAudioSourceNode render block writes samples via UnsafeMutableAudioBufferListPointer; LLMs often try to use AVAudioPCMBuffer instead which is the consumer-side API.

entries.goal: macOS Air Hockey — local two-player via MultipeerConnectivity. 500x700 SwiftUI window per player (run two instances on the same Wi-Fi). DISCOVERY: MCNearbyServiceAdvertiser (service type 'mc-airhockey') + MCNearbyServiceBrowser; advertise on launch, browser shows discovered peers in a SwiftUI List; tap to invite. Once paired, MCSession is established. GAMEPLAY: SpriteKit rink (mirrored top-down view), 2 paddles (one per peer), 1 puck. LOCAL paddle = follows mouse via .onContinuousHover. REMOTE paddle = position received via MCSession.send(_:toPeers:with:.unreliable) at 30Hz. Puck physics is HOST-AUTHORITATIVE (whoever invited is host); host computes puck position each frame and broadcasts via .reliable. GUEST does prediction + reconciliation: applies host updates, smooths via lerp. SCORING: puck enters either goal slot → +1 to opponent. First to 7 wins. SKPhysicsBody on paddles + puck for collisions. SKPhysicsContactDelegate detects goal entries via categoryBitMask. UI: status bar shows 'Connected to: <peer>' + score panel.
entries.notes: First MultipeerConnectivity entry. Native-mac angle is huge: web cannot do peer-to-peer LAN game discovery without a server. Trap: MCSession.send needs reliable vs unreliable choice — for 30Hz position updates, .unreliable is correct OR the queue overflows. Second trap: NSNetService bonjour requires 'Bonjour services' key in Info.plist OR the advertiser silently fails to register. Third trap: peers must be on the same SSID + multipeer needs NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription on macOS 11+.

entries.goal: macOS Defender — side-scrolling rescue shmup. 700x500 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Player ship SK_SpriteNode moves L/R via arrow keys (SKAction.move), thrust via 'X' key (continuous force), fires laser via Space (SKShapeNode bullets). Aliens spawn at edges, GRAB human-sprite dots from the bottom edge and carry them upward; if alien reaches the TOP, the human MUTATES and the alien becomes a 'mutant' (faster, more aggressive). Player goal: shoot aliens BEFORE they reach top + RESCUE falling humans by overlapping while their alien is dead (catch + carry to bottom). Background = 3 parallax layers via 3 SKAction.repeatForever moveBy at different speeds (foreground stars 1x, mid-stars 0.5x, far-stars 0.2x). 5 lives, increasing wave count + speed. Score top-left, lives top-right. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' (Cmd+N).
entries.notes: First parallax-scrolling entry. Defender's rescue-mechanic + mutant-transformation state machine is the L2 challenge. SKAction.repeatForever at 3 different speeds for parallax is a classic SpriteKit idiom LLMs sometimes miss — they'll instead update positions in update(_:) which is heavier.

entries.goal: macOS Joust — flap-mount jousting. 600x500 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Two-player local: P1 controls (WASD + Space-flap; W/S do nothing, A/D = horizontal force, Space = upward impulse), P2 controls (arrow keys + Return-flap). Both players are knights on ostrich-like flying mounts (SKShapeNode body). Floating platforms across the scene (SKPhysicsBody static). Lava bottom (any sprite touching = death). RULE: when two player-mounts collide (SKPhysicsContact), the HIGHER mount wins — lower mount loses 1 life and respawns. SKPhysicsContactDelegate + per-body altitude check (SKNode.position.y) inside didBegin(_:) determines winner. 5 lives each; last knight standing wins. OPTIONAL gamepad: GCController.controllers() falls back from second-player keys to a connected gamepad if exactly 1 connected. Score + lives HUD. CommandMenu Game > 'New Match' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Distinct mechanic: altitude-comparison-on-contact is unique to Joust. Two-player keyboard-split + optional gamepad is the input-plumbing challenge. LLM trap: didBegin(_:) is called on the contactDelegate from the physics thread — mutating SwiftUI @State directly causes warnings; must dispatch back to main.

entries.goal: macOS BurgerTime. 540x680 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Chef SK_SpriteNode climbs ladders + walks platforms (SKPhysicsBody dynamic, SKPhysicsBody.affectedByGravity = true on the chef, but not while on a ladder). Goal: drop burger components (top bun, lettuce, patty, bottom bun) from upper platforms by walking ACROSS them — each component falls 1 platform per crossing, ultimately to the bottom-of-stack tray (4 stacks per level). Enemies (Mr. Hot Dog, Mr. Pickle, Mr. Egg) spawn from the top, chase chef using GameplayKit GKAgent2D + GKGoal(toSeekAgent:) + GKGoal(toAvoidAgents:); chef can defeat enemies by (a) squashing them under a falling component (contact during component-fall = enemy removed), or (b) pepper-shoot (Spacebar, 3-shot inventory; pepper SKShapeNode briefly stuns a single enemy). 4 levels with increasing enemy count. Score top, lives bottom-right (3 lives). CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Falling-component physics + enemy-squash detection is the L2 spec. First GKAgent2D entry — GameplayKit's autonomous pathfinding agent. LLMs rarely reach for it; the test is whether they wire GKGoal-driven steering vs hand-rolled chase logic.

entries.goal: macOS Lode Runner — brick-dig platformer. 540x680 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Grid-based level (24x16 tiles): brick floors, ladders, ropes, hero spawn, gold piles, guard spawns. Hero (SK_SpriteNode) moves L/R, climbs ladders, hangs on ropes (NSEvent.keyDown for arrow keys). DIG: 'Q' digs the brick to the LEFT-BELOW the hero, 'E' digs to the RIGHT-BELOW; dug brick disappears for 3 seconds then regenerates (DispatchSourceTimer). Falling guard into a dug hole = guard drops in + (if the hole regenerates with guard inside) = guard dies. Hero collects gold piles (each contact +10 score); when ALL gold collected, an exit ladder appears spanning to top. GUARD AI: GameplayKit GKGridGraph traversable through 'walkable' tiles (floors + ladders + ropes); each guard pathfinds to hero using findPath(from:to:) on the GKGridGraph; on hole regenerate, also remove the dead guard's path nodes for the 3-second window. 5 increasing-difficulty levels.
entries.notes: Trap-via-dig mechanic + GKGridGraph pathfinding for guards — the L2 spec is whether the dig-floor-then-restore-after-3s timer integrates correctly with pathing graph mutation (need to call removeConnections + addConnections at the right times).

entries.goal: macOS Kaboom! with global mouse monitor. 480x680 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Bomber SK_SpriteNode walks L ↔ R at top edge, dropping bomb SKShapeNodes at irregular intervals. Player paddle (SKShapeNode 80x12 rectangle) at bottom moves with mouse-X position. CRITICAL: use CGEvent.tapCreate(tap: .cgSessionEventTap, place: .headInsertEventTap, options: .listenOnly, eventsOfInterest: 1 << CGEventType.mouseMoved.rawValue, callback: ..., userInfo: nil) so the paddle tracks the mouse EVEN WHEN the window has lost focus or the cursor is outside the window. Show that this is the difference from NSEventMonitor (which loses tracking on focus change) by adding a 'Click anywhere on screen' message at start — the user moves their mouse over a different app window and the paddle still tracks. Bombs fall under gravity; missed bombs explode + lose a paddle (3 paddles total). 8 increasing waves. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Native-only angle: global mouse monitor via CGEventTap is impossible on web. Most LLMs default to .onContinuousHover which only fires while window has focus; the prompt explicitly says 'global' to nudge them toward CGEventTap.tapCreate. Trap: CGEventTap requires Accessibility permissions OR will silently fail — the LLM should at least include a TCC-prompt comment.

entries.goal: macOS 9-Ball pool with 3D physics. 800x500 SwiftUI window hosting a SceneKit SCNView. SCNScene: rectangular pool table (felt SCNFloor with green material), 6 SCNNode pockets (trigger volumes, no rendered geometry), cue-ball + 9 numbered balls (each SCNSphere with SCNPhysicsBody dynamic, friction=0.4, restitution=0.85). Top-down camera. INPUT: hover the mouse around the cue-ball — cue-stick SCNNode (cylinder primitive) rotates around the cue-ball pointing toward mouse position (NSEvent mouseMoved). Click-and-hold (NSEvent leftMouseDown) + drag-back — cue-stick pulls back proportional to drag distance, vertical bar shows charge. Release (mouseUp) — apply impulse to cue-ball physicsBody.applyForce in the cue-stick direction proportional to charge. SCNPhysicsContactDelegate detects ball-pocket contact (categoryBitMask 'pocket'); pocketed-ball is removed from scene. 9-Ball rules: cue-ball MUST hit lowest-numbered ball first; pocketing the 9-ball wins. 2-player local turn-taking. CommandMenu Game > 'New Match' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Second SceneKit entry (after #135 bowling). Pool's chained-collision physics is the L3 challenge — LLMs often forget to tune restitution + friction; balls clack-clack and never stop. 9-Ball-first-hit rule + pocket-priority adds state machine complexity.

entries.goal: macOS Darts with CIFilter dartboard. 480x500 SwiftUI window. Dartboard rendered via Core Image: CIRadialGradient generates the cork (brown center → black outer), CISepiaTone tints the wood texture, CIBlend (.colorBurn) overlays the wedge sectors (20-sector polar division, alternating colors). The composed CIImage is rendered via MTKView (NSViewRepresentable wrapping a MetalKit view) for hardware-accelerated draw. INPUT: drag-pull-release in the bottom half (NSEvent leftMouseDragged) — dart-flight begins on mouseUp. Dart sprite flies via 0.3s animation toward the dartboard; during transit, the dart sprite's CIImage is composed with CIMotionBlur (radius=8, angle=flight-direction) for 'real' motion blur during flight. On landing, snap to a dartboard sector + score (treble ring 3x, double ring 2x, bullseye 50, bull 25, miss 0). 501-Down rules — must finish on a double. 3 darts per turn, alternating with 1 AI bot. Persist W/L tally + best-game.
entries.notes: First CoreImage / CIFilter entry. Native-mac angle: CIFilter chain on a Metal-backed view is the canonical fast-blur path; web equivalents (CSS filter:blur) are nowhere near as composable. CIRadialGradient + CIBlend chain is a classic 'looks better than it sounds' rendering technique.

entries.goal: macOS BMX-Trick scorer with replay scrub. 600x500 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit half-pipe scene. BMX-rider SK_SpriteNode side-scrolls a half-pipe (SKAction.repeatForever pan); player presses Space to JUMP off the lip (SKPhysicsBody applyImpulse upward), entering AIRBORNE state. While airborne, key-combos chain TRICK names: Q = Tabletop, W = Barspin, E = X-Up, R = Tailwhip; press combo (e.g. Q-W-E in 600ms = 'Tabletop → Barspin → X-Up'). Each frame of airtime captured to a buffer (replayBuffer.append((position, rotation, t))); trick name registered with timestamp. ON LANDING: airborne ends (SKPhysicsBody returns to ground), trick combo evaluated — score = (chain length × 100) + (airtime ms × 0.1). REPLAY MODE: bottom of window has an NSSlider (0...replayBuffer.count) — dragging the slider scrubs through the just-completed trick frame-by-frame, with the rider sprite repositioning per frame. SAVE REPLAY: Cmd+S → NSSavePanel writes the replayBuffer + trick timeline as JSON to ~/Library/Application Support/BMXReplays. CommandMenu Game > 'New Run' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Replay-buffer + scrub-via-NSSlider is the unique angle. First NSSlider-driven replay entry. Tests temporal state recording — most LLMs only model present-tick state. Trap: SwiftUI Slider gives a Double 0...1, but binding directly to replayBuffer.count needs CGFloat math.

entries.goal: macOS 1980s-arcade-style 10-pin bowling. 540x680 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit (deliberately NOT SceneKit — the 2D-arcade-flavor sibling of #135 SceneKit-bowling). Top-down lane: 10 pin SKShapeNodes at far end in standard triangle, ball SKShapeNode at the foul-line. INPUT: mouse-X positions ball at the foul-line. CLICK-HOLD (NSEvent leftMouseDown) sets initial power-meter (vertical bar fills at 50pt/sec); RELEASE (mouseUp) commits power = bar position; THROW = ball impulse forward. CRITICAL: while the LEFT mouse button is HELD (during charge phase), if the user RIGHT-MOUSE-BUTTON-DRAGS HORIZONTALLY (NSEvent rightMouseDragged), this APPLIES SPIN — the spin offset at release time becomes a SKPhysicsBody.angularVelocity that curves the ball trajectory. SKPhysicsBody on each pin (dynamic, restitution 0.5), 2D physics resolves pin-falls; SKPhysicsContactDelegate with categoryBitMask 'pin' counts. 10-frame standard scoring with spare/strike carry-over. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Distinct from #135 because 2D-arcade-style + right-mouse-button-drag for spin is a rare NSEvent path that LLMs almost always miss — they default to single-button input. Pairs naturally with #135 to compare 2D-vs-3D physics planner output.

entries.goal: macOS Pac-Man clone. 540x680 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Classic 28x31 grid maze drawn with SKShapeNode walls + dot SKShapeNodes (small) + 4 power-pellet SKShapeNodes (large, pulsing via SKAction.scale). Player Pac-Man SK_SpriteNode (yellow circle, mouth-open/close via SKAction.repeatForever) moves through corridors via arrow-keys (NSEvent.keyDown), CONTINUOUS-direction model (last-pressed direction is buffered + applied at next valid intersection). 4 ghosts (Blinky red, Pinky pink, Inky cyan, Clyde orange) each as a subclass of a base GhostNode — Blinky starts at the ghost-house exit (row 11, col 13) and immediately begins chasing; Pinky, Inky, Clyde start INSIDE the ghost house and are released via a per-ghost timer (Pinky 3s, Inky 7s, Clyde 11s after game start). Ghost movement: tile-based grid step, choose direction at each tile center that minimizes distance to target tile (no reverse except at dead ends). Ghost personality targets: Blinky=Pac-Man's tile; Pinky=4-tiles-ahead of Pac-Man; Inky=Blinky-offset mirror; Clyde=Pac-Man when >8 tiles away, scatter corner when close. CRITICAL WIRING in GameScene.update — call these every frame after moving Pac-Man: (1) Check Pac-Man's current grid tile; if tiles[row][col] == .dot: remove dotNodes[row][col], set tiles[row][col] = .empty, call gameState.eatDot(). (2) If tiles[row][col] == .pellet: same removal, call gameState.eatPowerPellet(), enter frightened mode on all ghosts. (3) For each ghost: if distance(ghost.position, pacMan.position) < 18pts: if ghost.isFrightened { ghost.isEaten = true; gameState.eatGhost() } else { handlePacManCaught() }. Power-pellet: all ghosts turn DARK BLUE (SKAction.colorize) for 8s; eaten ghosts return to spawn center and rejoin. Score top-left (10/dot, 50/power-pellet, 200-1600 chain on ghost-eat). 3 lives. Win when dotsRemaining==0. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N + 'Pause' Cmd+P (toggles SKView.isPaused).
entries.notes: First chase-grid + 4-distinct-AI-personality entry on macOS. GKAgent2D with per-ghost goal weights tests whether LLMs reach for GameplayKit's autonomous steering vs. hand-rolled BFS. Trap: continuous-direction buffering at intersections is a classic Pac-Man subtlety LLMs often skip — they implement immediate-turn-on-keypress which feels clunky.

entries.goal: macOS Asteroids — vector-style. 700x500 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Player ship = SKShapeNode triangle (3-vertex CGPath, white stroke, no fill, classic vector look). Movement: arrow-LEFT/RIGHT rotate via SKPhysicsBody.angularVelocity, arrow-UP applies thrust (SKPhysicsBody.applyForce in current heading direction), Spacebar fires bullet SKShapeNode (small white dot, lifetime 1.5s via SKAction.sequence([wait, removeFromParent])). All sprites use screen-wrap: when position.x < 0 → +screenWidth; > screenWidth → -screenWidth (same for y). Asteroids = irregular SKShapeNode polygons (6-10 random vertices on a circle + jitter) drifting at random velocities. Asteroid hit by bullet SPLITS into 2 smaller asteroids (3 size tiers: large→medium→small→destroyed). Ship-asteroid contact = lose-life (SKPhysicsContactDelegate). 3 lives. Hyperspace teleport via 'H' key (random repositioning, brief invuln). Saucer enemy SKShapeNode appears every 30s (SKAction.repeatForever wait+spawn) firing at player. Score (20/large, 50/medium, 100/small, 200/saucer). CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Vector-art via SKShapeNode (no SKSpriteNode at all) — the visual signature of Asteroids. Screen-wrap teleport on every sprite is the easy-to-forget-but-essential mechanic. LLM trap: bullets must use linearVelocity-based motion + lifetime, NOT update(_:) per-frame integration.

entries.goal: macOS Centipede. 540x680 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Mushroom field = grid of SKShapeNode mushrooms (random positions, 40% density on a 27x30 cell grid). Centipede = chain of 12 SKShapeNode segments (head-to-tail). Movement: enters from top, moves horizontally, drops down + reverses on hitting wall OR mushroom. Player ship SK_SpriteNode at bottom moves L/R via arrow keys, restricted to bottom 5 rows. Player fires 1-bullet-at-a-time SKShapeNode upward via Spacebar (one bullet on screen at a time — classic Centipede limit). HIT-LOGIC: bullet hits centipede segment → segment becomes a mushroom + centipede SPLITS into two independent chains at that point. Chain head gets bullet → segment dies cleanly. Mushrooms 4-shot to destroy. Spider SK_SpriteNode wanders bottom area zigzag (SKAction.sequence) eating mushrooms; killing scores by distance (300/600/900). Flea drops vertically planting mushrooms when count low. 3 lives. Score top, lives bottom-right. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Centipede-segment-split-on-hit is the unique mechanic: when bullet kills segment N of a 12-segment chain, you now have segments 1..N-1 + N+1..12 as TWO independent chains, each tracking its own head. LLM trap: many implementations just shorten the chain instead of splitting + spawning a new head.

entries.goal: macOS Galaga-style swoop shmup. 540x680 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Player ship SK_SpriteNode at bottom, arrow-keys L/R, Spacebar fires bullet SKShapeNode (max 2 onscreen). Enemy formation: 5 rows × 8 columns of alien SKShapeNodes. Aliens individually break formation in WAVES OF 2-4 and dive at the player using SKAction.followPath with a CGPath built from a Bézier curve (CGPath.addCurve(to:control1:control2:)) sweeping across the screen + back. After diving, surviving aliens return to their formation slot. Tractor-beam alien (boss-galaga) — yellow, larger, drops a beam SKShapeNode that captures player's ship for a bonus level (skip if too complex; emit a TODO). Player hit = lose-life. Killing a captured ship = score x2 for that ship's lifetime. 3 lives, 5 stages of increasing dive frequency. Score top-left. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: SKAction.followPath with a Bézier CGPath is the canonical SpriteKit way to do swoop attacks — most LLMs reach for update(_:)-based motion which fights SKAction's animation system. Tests whether the wizard finds the right primitive.

entries.goal: macOS Tetris. 460x720 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Standard 10-wide × 20-tall grid drawn with SKShapeNode block sprites. 7 tetromino pieces (I, O, T, S, Z, J, L) implemented as 4-cell shape arrays + per-piece SRS rotation kicks (Super Rotation System — wallkick offset table). Falling piece moves DOWN every gravity tick (SKAction.repeatForever wait + drop). Controls: arrow-LEFT/RIGHT shift, arrow-UP rotate-CW, Z rotate-CCW, arrow-DOWN soft-drop, SPACE hard-drop (instant + lock + score by distance), SHIFT hold-piece (1-use per piece, swap with held). NEXT-PIECE preview top-right (3-piece queue). LINE-CLEAR detection: scan rows after lock; remove full rows + drop above. Score: 1 line=100, 2=300, 3=500, 4 (Tetris)=800, T-spin bonuses optional. Levels speed up gravity every 10 lines. Game-over when new piece can't spawn. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N + 'Pause' Cmd+P + 'Hold' Shift.
entries.notes: SRS rotation with per-piece wallkick table is the canonical Tetris implementation, NOT naive 'rotate + check overlap + revert'. LLMs often skip wallkicks → I/J/L pieces feel jammed against walls. Hard-drop scoring + 4-line Tetris bonus + hold-piece are stake-in-the-ground correctness markers.

entries.goal: macOS Breakout. 540x680 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Brick wall at top: 8 rows × 10 columns of SKShapeNode bricks (top 2 rows red 5pts, next 2 orange 4pts, next 2 yellow 3pts, next 2 green/cyan 2pts). Paddle SK_SpriteNode at bottom moves L/R via mouse-X position OR arrow keys. Ball SKShapeNode (white circle) bounces using SKPhysicsBody (perfect-elastic, restitution=1.0, friction=0, linearDamping=0). Ball-paddle contact: deflection ANGLE depends on hit position relative to paddle center (left side → angle up-left, center → straight up, right side → up-right) — manually adjust ball.physicsBody.velocity in didBegin(_:contact:) by computing offset/half-width × max-deflection. Ball-brick contact: brick removed + score += brick.points + ball reflects off brick's hit-side. Speed-up at 8 hits, 16 hits, hitting top wall, hitting orange row. 3 lives. Win when all bricks cleared. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Manual paddle-deflection-by-position is the soul of Breakout: physics-only collision feels dead because the ball always reflects symmetrically. LLM trap: many implementations leave paddle as a pure physics body and the game is unwinnable because the ball gets stuck bouncing horizontally. Score-tier bricks + speed-up triggers are correctness markers.

entries.goal: macOS Lunar Lander. 700x500 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Lunar surface = SKShapeNode CGPath polyline drawn from a series of jagged-randomized terrain points + 1-3 FLAT landing pads (3-5x lander width, marked with a multiplier flag SKLabelNode '2X' / '5X' / '10X'). Lander SK_SpriteNode (small triangle/rocket SKShapeNode) starts at top with horizontal velocity. Continuous gravity via SKPhysicsBody.affectedByGravity = true (gravity = -1.6 m/s² scaled). Controls: arrow-UP applies upward thrust (SKPhysicsBody.applyForce; consumes fuel from a tank starting at 1000 units, drains 5/sec while held), arrow-LEFT/RIGHT rotate the lander (left/right rotation, then thrust direction follows facing). HUD: altitude (above terrain), horizontal velocity, vertical velocity, rotation angle, fuel-remaining, score. LANDING-LOGIC on contact: SAFE-LAND requires ALL of: vertical-vel < 5, horizontal-vel < 5, rotation < 5° from upright, contact with landing-pad (not terrain). On safe-land, score += pad-multiplier × fuel-remaining. On crash (any other contact), explosion (SKEmitterNode), -1 ship. 5 ships. CommandMenu Game > 'New Mission' Cmd+N + 'Reset Terrain' Cmd+R.
entries.notes: L3 — multi-axis physics (gravity + thrust-vector + rotation + fuel) + procedurally-generated terrain via CGPath polyline + landing-pad-multiplier scoring. SKPhysicsBody.affectedByGravity is the canonical primitive but most LLMs reach for manual integration. Trap: the win-condition predicate (4-clauses ANDed) often gets simplified to vertical-velocity-only.

entries.goal: macOS Marble Madness. 600x600 SwiftUI window hosting SceneKit (NOT SpriteKit). Isometric-ish 3D scene: SCNView with SCNScene containing a marble SCNSphere (radius 0.5, SCNPhysicsBody.dynamic, mass=1, friction=0.3), tilted-flat course geometry built from SCNBox platforms + ramps. Course-1 layout: 3 connected platforms with ramps, START square at top, GOAL square at bottom, ENEMIES = 1-2 SCNCone 'Slinky' enemies that wander toward marble (SCNAction.sequence). Camera = SCNNode at fixed isometric angle (eulerAngles = (-30°,45°,0°), positioned to frame the course). Tilt-via-keys: arrow-LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN apply IMPULSE to marble's physicsBody in world XZ axes (NOT camera-relative — easier to reason about). Falling off the platform = lose-life + respawn at start. Reaching goal-trigger SCNNode = next course (3 courses, increasing complexity). HUD label SwiftUI overlay (marble velocity + lives + course #). Lives=5. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: L3 — first SceneKit-based game in the macOS arcade catalog (existing macos-l2-games-sports-bowling-135 is also SceneKit but ball/pin physics-vertical, not isometric platforms). Tests SCNPhysicsBody dynamic vs. kinematic for course geometry, isometric-camera framing, marble-vs-platform friction tuning. Often missed: making platforms STATIC physicsBodies — if dynamic, the marble pushes the entire course.

entries.goal: macOS Bomberman. 540x680 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. 13×11 grid of cells with 3 cell types: empty, indestructible-wall (every other-other cell forming a fixed grid), destructible-block (random 60% fill of remaining cells, dropped on top of player-spawn quadrant). Player SK_SpriteNode (any color, classic egg/bomberman shape) starts top-left, moves on grid via arrow keys (LERP between cells via SKAction.move(to:duration:0.2), one cell per keypress with input buffering). SPACEBAR drops a bomb SKShapeNode at player's current cell (max 1 bomb at a time at start). Bomb fuse = 3 seconds (SKAction.sequence([wait(3.0), explode-action])). Explosion = + shape: bomb cell + 1 cell each direction (UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT) — flames stop at indestructible-wall, destroy 1 destructible-block per direction. Power-ups (random drop from destroyed blocks): +1 max-bomb, +1 explosion-radius, +1 walk-speed. Enemies: 2 simple wandering enemies (random direction, reverse on wall; killed by flame). Player dies on flame contact OR enemy contact. 3 lives, 1 level (placeholder), goal = clear all destructible blocks + kill all enemies. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Grid-locked movement + +-shaped explosion propagation + power-up state (max-bomb count, radius, walk-speed) is the spec. LLM trap: bomb-explosion timing via Timer instead of SKAction-on-bomb-node — the moment you nuke the bomb mid-fuse (e.g., chain explosion) the Timer keeps firing. SKAction tied to the node cleans up correctly.

entries.goal: macOS Tron Light Cycles. 700x500 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Top-down arena with 1px black border + dark-grid background. Two cycles: P1 (cyan) starts left moving right, P2 (orange) starts right moving left. Both move forward CONTINUOUSLY at constant speed via SKAction.repeatForever moveBy. Each cycle leaves a trail = SKShapeNode cells (1×1 per game-tick) accumulating in a parent node. Trails are walls. Crashing into ANY trail (own or opponent's) OR arena edge = lose-round. P1 turns: A=left-90°-relative-to-heading, D=right-90° (heading-relative, not absolute). P2 turns: arrow-LEFT / arrow-RIGHT (heading-relative). Best-of-5 rounds, draw if both crash same frame. Round end shows winner + 'Press Space to continue'. Optional AI mode (P2 = computer): 3-cell-lookahead avoidance + biased toward arena center. Score HUD top, current round indicator. CommandMenu Game > 'New Match' Cmd+N + 'Toggle AI' Cmd+I.
entries.notes: Heading-relative-90°-turn (NOT absolute compass turn) is the Tron-cycle control feel. Trail is wall — both cycles' trails coexist in collision check. LLM trap: trail-cell collision via SKPhysics often fails because tons of small cells = physics-engine soup; faster: maintain a Set<GridCell> for occupied cells + check each tick by index. Worth flagging if LLM picks the heavy path.

entries.goal: macOS ColecoVision Football. 600x460 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Side-view football field — small horizontal pitch (NOT vertical/top-down). Player offense (BLUE, 4 SKShapeNode players in basic formation) starts at left 30-yard line; defense (RED, 4 SKShapeNode players in 2-deep zone) at right. PRE-SNAP: arrow keys pick play (UP=pass-deep, DOWN=run-up-the-middle, LEFT=run-sweep, RIGHT=pass-short); SPACE = snap. Post-snap: WASD moves the ball-carrier (or QB on passes); on pass plays, RELEASE space mid-throw to launch the ball — receiver SKShapeNode runs an automatic route, catches if not covered. Defenders chase with a simple GKAgent2D toSeekAgent + GKGoal-toAvoidObstacles for blockers. Tackle = SKPhysicsContactDelegate didBegin (carrier-vs-defender contact). 1st-down chain: 10 yards in 4 downs. Touchdown when carrier crosses right end zone. Score top, down/distance/quarter HUD bottom. 4 quarters of 60s each (game-clock SKAction.repeatForever). CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N + 'Pause' Cmd+P.
entries.notes: Side-view (NOT top-down) is the ColecoVision signature — the field scrolls horizontally. Pre-snap-then-snap with play-selection is the early-football idiom. LLM trap: passing-mechanic — many implementations have the ball as a child of the QB sprite and forget to release it as an independent SKPhysicsBody when SPACE released; result is the ball never travels. Receiver-route automation tests whether the LLM reaches for SKAction.followPath.

entries.goal: macOS Atari 2600-style Basketball. 480x540 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Half-court top-down view: hoop at top center, baseline at bottom. Two stick-figure players: PLAYER (cyan) vs CPU (orange). Ball (yellow circle) starts held by player. PLAYER INPUT: WASD = move 1 cell per keypress on a 12x10 grid, SPACE = shoot if near hoop zone OR pass if far, F = pump-fake. BALL POSSESSION WIRING — CRITICAL: a player 'holds' the ball when `distanceSq(player.position, ball.position) < 24*24`. When the ball is loose, whichever player reaches it first picks it up (check in update loop: `if !ball.isHeld && distanceSq(p.position, ball.position) < holdRadius*holdRadius { p.hasBall = true; ball.isHeld = true; ball.holder = p }`). SHOOT WIRING: when SPACE pressed and player.hasBall and player is in hoop zone (top half of court): launch ball along arc toward hoop via SKAction.sequence([parabolic move, check-at-hoop]). Score 2pts if ball enters hoop rect (y near hoopY, x within ±20pts of hoopX). MISS: ball bounces off backboard rectangle, becomes loose. CPU defender: simple chase — move toward ball.holder each tick at CPU speed (no GKAgent2D needed, just `cpuPos += normalize(ballHolderPos - cpuPos) * cpuSpeed * dt`). STEAL: if CPU within 20pts of player while player is not actively shooting, ball becomes loose. SHOT-CLOCK: 24 seconds per possession, SKLabelNode countdown; turnover if expires. Quarters: 4×30 seconds. Score top-left, shot-clock top-right. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N.
entries.notes: Half-court 1v1 keeps it tractable. Step-per-keypress (NOT continuous WASD held) is the Atari-2600 signature — most LLMs default to held-keys; the prompt says 'move 1 step per keypress'. Backboard rebound + 3-point arc + shot-clock are correctness markers. Pump-fake is a deliberate trap: the LLM either implements it (defender freezes 1 step) or skips.

entries.goal: macOS Atari Adventure-reboot — Adventure×Rogue×Mario hybrid. 720x540 SwiftUI window hosting SpriteKit. Top-down 2D world map of 4 connected SCREENS (rooms): (1) Yellow Castle (player spawn, locked gate at south, key needed), (2) White Castle (sword inside, dragon guardian), (3) Black Castle (chalice goal — bring this back to Yellow Castle to win), (4) Catacombs maze (procedurally-generated rogue-like grid via GameplayKit GKGridGraph; fog-of-war revealed in player's 3-cell radius). Player SKShapeNode square (cyan) walks via WASD + arrows. Items as SKShapeNodes: yellow KEY, white KEY, black KEY, SWORD, CHALICE, MAGNET (attracts items), BRIDGE (drops over walls). Pick up = walk over (auto-pickup). Inventory shows top-right (max 1 item carried — drop with E to swap, like original Adventure). DRAGONS: 3 SKSpriteNode dragons (red/yellow/green), each in a different room, GKAgent2D pathfinding toward player; on contact, if player has SWORD = dragon dies; if not = player eaten + game-over → respawn at last castle. KEYS unlock matching colored gates between rooms (SKShapeNode rectangle removed on contact when player carries matching key). MARIO TOUCH: each castle has decorative parapet SKShapeNode towers + flag-on-pole at top + brick-textured (SKAction.colorize via grid-pattern shader-ish — just stripes are fine). The maze level uses Rogue-style: random walls each game, hidden bat enemy that picks up items + relocates them (Adventure feature). Goal: bring CHALICE back to Yellow Castle's center → win screen with elapsed time. CommandMenu Game > 'New Game' Cmd+N + 'Drop Item' (E) + 'Map' Cmd+M (toggles full-map overlay).
entries.notes: L3 — 4 connected screens with state (room transitions preserve global game state), inventory swap-only mechanic (Adventure's signature constraint), procedural maze (rogue), Mario-castle decorative trim, bat-relocates-items (Adventure's most-quoted gameplay quirk). The chained-room state machine + GKGridGraph procgen + bat-AI item-stealing combine to test multi-system coherence. LLM trap: forgets that Adventure's INVENTORY = 1-item-only (modern devs default to backpack-style); the prompt says 'max 1 item carried — drop with E to swap'. Bat is the most distinctive feature — without it the game feels like generic top-down ARPG. Lifelong Atari fans will recognize.
